what_it_does: Implements a reusable, interactive graph viewer instrument for the DNA surface. It renders a graph.json substrate as a pannable, zoomable, columnar geometric projection; supports folder drill-down, file selection cards, type highlighting, address filtering, camera framing, and responsive reflow.
observations: Attaches three public functions to the global DNA namespace: renderGraph, bindGraph, mountGraph.
Builds a containment tree from flat graph.json clusters, reconstructing intermediate directories from node paths and computing descendant file counts.
Fetches /dna/tokens.json for type_hues and builds a type-to-CSS-color resolver with bronze fallbacks.
Defers actual geometry/layout generation to DNA.org.graph while this file manages state, events, and camera.
Pointer-driven pan, two-finger pinch, wheel zoom anchored at the cursor, plus recenter/zoom buttons.
Legend recounts visible file types per drilled view and toggles type highlighting.
Card shows plain-English relationships with navigable chips for outgoing/incoming links and folder children.
Uses ResizeObserver or window resize to recompute layout for the live stage size.
State is stored on the element under _gr, including path, pan, zoom, selection, filter, and highlight.
standouts: Cursor-anchored zoom math keeps the substrate point under the pointer fixed while scaling.
Camera framing fitToDepth centers the parent column and the opened folder while bounding zoom for readability.
Directory tree is reconstructed from flat file paths rather than supplied explicitly.
Type hue resolver flattens category/type mappings and maps theme names to CSS custom properties.
conventions: Follows the DNA render/bind/mount component shape.
Uses data-role attributes and class selectors to query generated DOM.
Builds HTML via innerHTML with an esc helper for text content.
Uses event delegation for legend and card interactions.
Calls setPointerCapture for reliable pointer dragging.
concerns: mountGraph replaces container.innerHTML, destroying any pre-existing content.
Rebind only disconnects the ResizeObserver and resize handler; other event listeners attached during bindGraph are not explicitly removed.
Hard-coded default URLs /registry/graph.json and /dna/tokens.json with no cache-busting or request options.
No accessibility live-region announcements for view changes or selection.
If DNA.org.graph or DNA.iconSVG are missing, the viewer degrades silently but may produce empty output.
notes: This file is the interactive surface lens: it turns a flat registry graph into a navigable folder/file substrate with camera movement, filtering, and readable context cards.
questions: What exact geometry contract does DNA.org.graph expect and what keys does it write to the out object?
What is the full schema of graph.json clusters, nodes, edges, types, and root metadata?
How are type icon names resolved when DNA.iconSVG is present, and what fallback exists if it is absent?
Is the legend intended to support filtering in addition to highlighting?
What should happen when a card chip points to a node not present in the current graph?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a live, reusable, responsive graph instrument with render/bind/mount shape, pan/zoom, drill-down, cards, legend, and type coloring.
apparent_themes: Addressed design tokens for color (type hues from tokens.json).
Live, responsive geometric projection of a code substrate.
Camera-as-movement with eased transitions and cursor-anchored zoom.
intention_signals: To make a codebase's containment and dependency structure explorable through a spatial, gesture-driven interface where folders are depths, files are nodes, and relationships are presented as readable, navigable cards.
novelty: The combination of path-based columnar layout, cursor-anchored zoom math, per-view legend counts, and directory-tree reconstruction from flat file paths within a single reusable DNA instrument.
contribution: A self-contained interactive graph viewer that reconstructs a directory tree from flat nodes, frames a camera to the current drill depth, drives pointer-based pan/zoom, highlights types, and renders navigable file cards for any graph.json substrate.
summary_for_embedding: graph-view.js implements DNA.renderGraph, DNA.bindGraph, and DNA.mountGraph, an interactive, pannable, zoomable graph viewer that reconstructs a directory tree from flat graph.json nodes, colors nodes by type hues from tokens.json, and provides drill-down folders, filtering, legend highlighting, and plain-English relationship cards.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a reusable surface instrument for exploring a code/design substrate as a geometric, interactive graph with movement, filtering, and readable context.

symbols.does: HTML-escapes a small set of characters for safe text insertion.
symbols.description: Converts null or undefined to an empty string and escapes &, <, >, and " to HTML entities; used when building innerHTML strings.

symbols.does: Returns the last path segment of an address.
symbols.description: Splits an id on "/" and returns the final component; used for breadcrumb labels and card titles.

symbols.does: Builds a type-to-color resolver from token type_hues data.
symbols.description: Flattens category/type mappings and theme names into a function that returns CSS variable colors for node types, with bronze fallbacks for unmapped, folder, and ghost types.

symbols.does: Returns a CSS variable reference for a palette theme primary color.
symbols.description: Produces var(--palette-<name>-primary, var(--bronze)) so type hues resolve through host-injected design tokens.

symbols.does: Reconstructs a containment tree from flat graph.json clusters.
symbols.description: Creates directory nodes for intermediate path segments, links files to their parent directories, and computes descendant file counts for folders.

symbols.does: Creates or returns a directory node for a given address.
symbols.description: Builds a dir node with label, type, icon, and file_count; the root address "." uses data.root.label or "root".

symbols.does: Registers a child under a parent in the tree.
symbols.description: Pushes the child into childrenOf[parent] if not already present, establishing parent-child edges.

symbols.does: Returns the children array for an address from the built tree.
symbols.description: Looks up tree.childrenOf[addr], defaulting to an empty array; used to resolve visible columns and folder contents.

symbols.does: Computes the columns of nodes shown for the current drill path.
symbols.description: Returns an array where index 0 is root's children and each subsequent index is the children of the corresponding path segment.

symbols.does: Applies current pan and zoom to the pan container via CSS transform.
symbols.description: Sets pan.style.transform to translate(x,y) scale(z) using state st.pan and st.zoom.

symbols.does: Smoothly transitions the pan/zoom transform over about 480ms.
symbols.description: Adds a CSS easing class, applies the transform, and removes the class after a timeout so later drags remain instant.

symbols.does: Resets text filter and type highlight state.
symbols.description: Clears st.filter, st.hl, and the filter input value.

symbols.does: Sorts a list of nodes with directories first, then alphabetically.
symbols.description: Returns a new array sorted by isDir flag then by id string localeCompare.

symbols.does: Frames the camera around the parent column and opened folder.
symbols.description: Computes zoom based on sibling content size and positions the pan so the parent level is visible and the opened child is centered, optionally easing.

symbols.does: Renders the type legend with counts for the current view.
symbols.description: Tallies visible file types across visible columns and builds highlightable buttons with type icons and counts.

symbols.does: Re-renders the graph stage, legend, and breadcrumb metadata.
symbols.description: Measures the stage, calls DNA.org.graph to generate geometry/HTML, stores geometry output, updates the legend and back button, and refreshes the subtitle breadcrumb.

symbols.does: Drills into a folder by setting the active path to its ancestor chain.
symbols.description: Splits the folder address into path segments, clears selection/card, re-renders, and eases the camera to the new depth.

symbols.does: Moves one level up toward root.
symbols.description: Removes the last path segment, clears selection/card, re-renders, and eases the camera.

symbols.does: Builds a small navigable button for a node id.
symbols.description: Returns HTML for a button with the node's icon and base name, tagged with data-nav for card relationship navigation.

symbols.does: Builds a labeled row of navigable chips capped at eight items.
symbols.description: Renders a section label and up to eight chip buttons, with a +N indicator for overflow.

symbols.does: Displays an information card for a folder or file.
symbols.description: For folders shows file count and direct children; for files shows address, reference counts, outgoing/incoming chips, and summary.

symbols.does: Hides the card and clears selection if any.
symbols.description: Sets the card hidden, empties its HTML, and if a node is selected clears selection and re-renders.

symbols.does: Jumps to a node from a card chip.
symbols.description: Clears filters, opens a folder directly, or opens the file's parent folder and selects the file with its card.

symbols.does: Clamps and applies a zoom level.
symbols.description: Restricts zoom to [0.3, 3.4] and calls applyTransform.

symbols.does: Zooms centered on a screen point while keeping that point fixed over the substrate.
symbols.description: Calculates new pan coordinates using the cursor position and zoom ratio, removes easing, and applies the transform.

symbols.does: Records the initial pointer position and pan offset for dragging.
symbols.description: Stores pointer coordinates and current st.pan values at the start of a one-finger drag.

symbols.does: Cleans up pointer state when a pointer is released.
symbols.description: Removes the pointer from the active map, ends pinch if fewer than two pointers remain, and transitions back to one-finger panning if one pointer remains.

symbols.does: Computes Euclidean distance between two points.
symbols.description: Uses Math.hypot to return the distance between points with x and y properties, used for pinch zoom delta.
